appliedUpdates.description: Added rust_test and rust_build commands so future Rust workers can use standardized cargo invocations without constructing manual --manifest-path arguments.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Add PYTHONPATH workaround to rust-worker skill: On Windows worktrees, 'uv run pytest -q' may fail with ModuleNotFoundError unless PYTHONPATH is set to <worktree>/src. Document the workaround: 'cmd /c set PYTHONPATH=<worktree>\src&& uv run --directory <worktree> pytest -q'.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: Feature rust-zero-copy-replace worker spent multiple retries discovering this workaround. It's documented in mission AGENTS.md but not in the rust-worker skill itself.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Add Windows-specific note to rust-worker skill step '1.2 Initialize Environment': .factory/init.sh is not executable on native Windows. Workers should use 'uv pip install -e ".[dev,ast,nlp]"' directly instead.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: Feature rust-zero-copy-replace worker deviated from init.sh usage because it's a bash script not executable on native Windows. Worker's own skillFeedback confirms this gap.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Document the source-level architectural test pattern used in rust_core/tests/test_replace.rs: grepping Rust source files to enforce invariants (e.g., verifying mmap usage, absence of std::fs::read in replace path). This pattern is useful for preventing architectural regression in performance-critical paths.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: Feature rust-zero-copy-replace introduced test_replace_path_uses_mutable_memmap_instead_of_full_file_reads which reads the Rust source at test time to verify the mmap invariant. Novel and useful but potentially brittle — worth documenting as an accepted practice with known limitations.
